title: Seborrheic dermatitis
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.
red_flags.rationale: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful worsening lesions, spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever or systemic illness, painful rapidly worsening eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, and clinician-documented worsening or return fields.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports clinician-reviewed family history and scalp or prior-treatment context without inferring a differential.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed family history in psoriasis context and KOH or fungal-culture documentation when tinea corporis is a differential, without inferring either diagnosis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis

medication.text: Supports documenting skin-care, dandruff-shampoo, eyelid-care, clinician-stated topical treatment, and follow-up discussion without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.
medication.rationale: Supports clinician-documented skin care, dandruff shampoo, topical-treatment decisions, and follow-up without medicine names or doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting skin-care, dandruff-shampoo, eyelid-care, clinician-stated topical treatment, and follow-up discussion without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Seborrheic Dermatitis

exact_section.heading: Section 8 — clinician-led advice and pharmacological treatment

follow_up.text: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.
follow_up.rationale: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial itch and scale, greasy plaques, erythema, distribution, course, and clinician-reviewed history.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports seborrheic-dermatitis and dandruff terminology, scalp or facial scale, pruritus, greasy plaques, erythema, scalp, eyebrow, nasolabial, eyelid, ear, trunk, fold, relapsing, stress, and seasonal context.

source_limitations: Mucosal involvement, immunocompromise, fixed duration presets, fixed two-to-four-week review, and broad autonomous management remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer seborrheic dermatitis, infection, psoriasis, or tinea or recommend shampoo, topical medicine, or referral unless clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
